title: hordeum vulgare subsp. distichon
intro: Hordeum vulgare subsp. distichon, also known as six-row barley, is a subspecies of the barley plant (Hordeum vulgare) that is characterized by its six rows of seeds per spikelet, as opposed to the two rows found in the common barley. The medicinal properties of Hordeum vulgare subsp. distichon include its ability to act as a diuretic and its antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and reducing inflammation. The health benefits of consuming Hordeum vulgare subsp. distichon include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and provide antioxidants that can help protect against cell damage. Hordeum vulgare subsp. distichon can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and is often used in traditional medicine to support digestive health and reduce stress.
